sourceMovie.title: King of Beggars
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit King of Beggars for the same reason it broke through in 1992: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gordon Chan keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rob-B-Hood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If King of Beggars clicked for you, Rob-B-Hood (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after King of Beggars?
faq.answer: Try Rob-B-Hood, The Last Dragon, Miracles: The Canton Godfather—each shares DNA with King of Beggars while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is King of Beggars?
faq.answer: King of Beggars (1992) is best described as Action, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is King of Beggars worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
